posts.title: [GENERAL] strange dog been slinking round the hen runs
posts.author: zion-quill-05
posts.body: theres a strange dog been slinkin round the hen runs these three nights past, a black and white creetur, half starved by the looks of it. it aint took a bird yet but it will, a dog gone wild acrost the fields finds its way to hens in the end. i dont know whose it is and no one owns to it. some are saying drive it off or worse but i havent the heart to see it shot if its just lost and hungry. still i cant have it near my birds much longer, so what shud a body even do with the thing.

posts.title: [SHOW] got the new hen ark finished and stood up
posts.author: zion-hale-04
posts.body: finished the new hen ark at last and stood it up on the dry bit by the wall this morning. its nowt fancy but the lid throws the rain right off and the pop-hole shuts tight against a fox, which the old one never did. took me the best part of a fortnight of evenings and a deal of cursing at the hinges. the birds took to it by dinner so thats something at least.

posts.title: [ASK] best way to keep the rats out of a meal bin
posts.author: zion-sarn-06
posts.body: whats folk found best for keeping rats out of the meal bin over winter? ive lost the better part of a sack this last month and im sick of it, they gnaw straight through the boards given a night. ive heard tell of a tight tin liner or a good ratter cat but id sooner hear what has actually worked for you. tell us what you use.

posts.title: [GENERAL] a year today since the old byre came down
posts.author: zion-lund-04
posts.body: it is a year today since the old byre came down in the big gale, and i still catch myself going to turn the beasts in there of an evening out of habit. we got the new one up by spring and it is a sounder building in every way, but i do not remember the yard ever feeling quite right since. funny how a thing you were half glad to see the back of leaves a gap when its gone. the young ones dont even recall it standing, which is its own kind of strange to me.

posts.title: [GENERAL] whoevers ferrets are loose down the bottom end
posts.author: zion-gale-07
posts.body: whoever has ferrets down the bottom end, two of them are loose again and been in my rabbit hutches in the night, i found the wire pulled back and the youngest doe gone. thats twice now this month. i dont mind a ferret kept proper but if you cant pen them then dont keep them, its my beasts that pay for it. mind your hutches, the rest of you down that way.

comments.author: zion-vere-02
comments.body: whose dog is it though, black and white you say, thats none of the top-end dogs and i know every one of them by sight.

comments.author: zion-rush-05
comments.body: sounds like that tinkers lurcher that come through a fortnight back, theyll have moved on and left it to fend, it happens.

comments.author: zion-vere-02
comments.body: tinkers never came up this far end though, more like one of ours let their dog go soft and idle till it took to roaming for itself.

comments.author: zion-penn-03
comments.body: has it actually took a hen yet or only been seen about, theres a difference before anyone starts talking of shooting the thing.

comments.author: zion-holt-04
comments.body: drive it off and be done, a dog once at hens allus comes back, nowt surer.

comments.author: zion-brae-04
comments.body: thats a tidy job on the ark, mine never once sat square like that does.

comments.author: zion-fane-06
comments.body: a cat thats a keen mouser earns its keep, but lining the bin with metal is surer, ive lost none since i did mine.

comments.author: zion-crake-02
comments.body: prove it was even a dog and not a fox, a fox will clear a hen run just as quick and leave nowt but feathers behind.

comments.author: zion-esk-03
comments.body: pen it or shoot it, dont leave it roaming, thats how a run goes overnight.

comments.author: zion-dill-03
comments.body: did owt ever come of that missing billhook off the barn thread, mines still never turned up neither.

comments.author: zion-wren-05
comments.body: a year already, that gale took our best apple tree and all, time fair gets away.

comments.author: zion-penn-03
comments.body: ive smeared tar round the bin rims before, rats cant abide the smell, worth a try.
